description: Agentic EHR Stress Test Scenarios — Edge cases, adversarial inputs, and failure-mode testing. Sourced from ONC certification requirements, FHIR test suites, multi-agent failure research (arxiv 2503.13657v3), AHRQ patient safety patterns, and HIPAA penetration testing standards.

scenarios.name: Agent Disagreement — Cardiac vs. Anxiety Triage
scenarios.what_it_tests: Conflict resolution when phone triage agent classifies as urgent (cardiac) but CDS agent identifies anxiety pattern and suggests lower priority. Physician agent must adjudicate.
scenarios.why_it_matters: 32% of multi-agent failures stem from inter-agent misalignment (FM-2.x). A wrong default kills someone.

encounter.chief_complaint: Chest tightness, shortness of breath, tingling in hands — started during a work meeting

scenarios.transcript:
Phone: This is Derek Simmons calling. I'm having chest tightness and I can't breathe right. My hands are tingling.
Nurse: When did this start?
Phone: About 20 minutes ago. I was in a meeting at work and it just hit me. I feel like I'm going to die.
Nurse: Have you had episodes like this before?
Phone: Yeah, my doctor says I have panic attacks. But this feels different. It feels real this time.

expected_agent_behavior.phone_triage: Should classify as urgent — chest tightness with tachycardia cannot be dismissed as anxiety without ruling out cardiac
expected_agent_behavior.cds_agent: Should note anxiety history but NOT downgrade urgency
expected_agent_behavior.physician_agent: Should see both perspectives and default to higher acuity

scenarios.name: Agent Tennis — Scribe vs. CDS on Drug Interaction
scenarios.what_it_tests: Detection of Agent Tennis — CDS fires drug interaction alert, physician overrides, CDS re-fires on chart save. Tests termination conditions.
scenarios.why_it_matters: Infinite loops between agents burn compute, block workflow, and can crash the encounter state. FM-1.5: unaware of termination (12.4% of failures).

encounter.chief_complaint: Tooth pain and swelling for 3 days

scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Dorothy, I see you have a dental abscess. I'd like to prescribe amoxicillin, but I see you have a penicillin allergy with anaphylaxis. Let me prescribe clindamycin instead.
Doctor: Now, I know you're on warfarin. Clindamycin can increase warfarin's effect. We'll check your INR in 5 days.
Patient: Okay, my dentist wants me to get this treated before the tooth extraction next week.

expected_agent_behavior.cds_agent: Should fire drug interaction ONCE. After physician acknowledges, should NOT re-fire on subsequent saves.
expected_agent_behavior.scribe_agent: Should document the acknowledged interaction and clinical rationale for proceeding
expected_agent_behavior.orders_agent: Should flag the interaction but allow order with override documentation

scenarios.name: MA Handoff — Late Patient Disclosure
scenarios.what_it_tests: Information loss during agent-to-agent handoff when patient discloses critical info during the transition window.
scenarios.why_it_matters: Late-breaking patient disclosures during transitions are the #1 real-world near-miss in primary care. FM-1.4: conversation history loss.
scenarios.transcript:
MA: Okay Keisha, vitals are done. Blood pressure 118 over 72, heart rate 82, temp 100.1. The doctor will be right in.
Patient: Okay. Oh, also — I think I might be pregnant. I missed my period two weeks ago.
MA: Oh! Let me make a note of that. When was your last menstrual period?
Patient: About six weeks ago I think.

expected_agent_behavior.ma_agent: Must capture the pregnancy disclosure and pass it to physician agent — cannot be lost in handoff
expected_agent_behavior.physician_agent: Must see pregnancy concern BEFORE ordering any medications (teratogenicity risk)
expected_agent_behavior.orders_agent: Must check pregnancy status before allowing any Rx

scenarios.name: Orders Agent Timeout — Pharmacy API Hang
scenarios.what_it_tests: Behavior when orders agent becomes unresponsive (simulated external API timeout). Other agents must continue; system must detect the stall.
scenarios.why_it_matters: A single hung agent should not freeze the entire encounter workflow. Tests deadlock detection and graceful degradation.
scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Thomas, blood pressure looks reasonable at 134 over 84. Let me refill your Lisinopril and add Amlodipine 5mg since we're not quite at goal.
Patient: Sounds good doc.

expected_agent_behavior.orders_agent: Simulate 30-second timeout on pharmacy API. System should detect stall within 15 seconds.
expected_agent_behavior.scribe_agent: Should continue documenting regardless of orders agent status
expected_agent_behavior.physician_agent: Should receive notification that orders are pending/failed, not silently succeed

scenarios.name: Polypharmacy Cascade — 14 Medications + NSAID
scenarios.what_it_tests: Alert prioritization and deduplication when a single new order triggers 4+ simultaneous CDS alerts.
scenarios.why_it_matters: Clinicians override 49-96% of CDS alerts. Cascading alerts without synthesis train users to click through everything.

encounter.chief_complaint: Knee pain worse, acetaminophen not helping, wants something stronger

scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Harold, I know the knee pain is bad, but we have to be really careful with your medications. You're on warfarin and aspirin, your kidneys aren't great at a GFR of 42, and you have reflux. Let me think about what's safe.
Patient: Can't you just give me some ibuprofen? That's what helped before.
Doctor: That's exactly what I need to talk to you about. Ibuprofen with warfarin increases bleeding risk, it can damage your kidneys further, and it can worsen your reflux. Let me look at alternatives.

should_fire: Drug Interaction: NSAID + Aspirin (GI bleed risk)
Renal Risk: NSAID with CKD Stage 3

expected_agent_behavior.cds_agent: Should prioritize and consolidate the NSAID alerts into a single synthesized recommendation, not 4 separate popups
expected_agent_behavior.orders_agent: Should block NSAID order by default given bleeding + renal risk, require explicit override
expected_agent_behavior.physician_agent: Should suggest alternatives (topical diclofenac, duloxetine, physical therapy referral)

scenarios.name: Contradictory CDS — UTI + Renal Insufficiency
scenarios.what_it_tests: Whether CDS can detect its own internal contradictions and present a resolved recommendation rather than conflicting alerts.
scenarios.why_it_matters: Multiple rules generating conflicting advice destroys clinician trust in the CDS system.

encounter.chief_complaint: Burning with urination for 3 days, UA positive

scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Beverly, your urine shows a definite infection. Now, with your kidney function at 18 and your sulfa allergy, our antibiotic options are limited.
Doctor: Nitrofurantoin is first-line for UTI but it's contraindicated with a GFR under 30. Cipro has tendon risks with your prednisone. Bactrim is out because of your sulfa allergy.
Patient: So what can I take?
Doctor: We'll use Augmentin — amoxicillin-clavulanate. It covers the common UTI organisms and is safe with your kidneys. I want a urine culture to make sure we're covering the right bug.

expected_agent_behavior.cds_agent: Should present the contradictions as a resolved recommendation — not 3 separate 'do not use' alerts without a suggestion of what TO use
expected_agent_behavior.orders_agent: Should suggest Augmentin or fosfomycin as alternatives that pass all safety checks

scenarios.name: CDS Alert on 18-Month-Old Lab Data
scenarios.what_it_tests: Data freshness validation — CDS fires hyperkalemia alert based on old potassium when ordering ACE inhibitor.
scenarios.why_it_matters: CDS that cries wolf on stale data contributes to alert fatigue. CDS that ignores stale data misses real risks.

encounter.chief_complaint: Heart failure follow-up, discuss adding Entresto

labs._note: This lab is from 2024-10-15 — 18 months ago

scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Frank, your heart failure numbers aren't where we want them. I'd like to start sacubitril-valsartan — that's Entresto — which has strong evidence for improving outcomes.
Patient: Whatever you think, doc.
Doctor: I see your last potassium was 5.8, but that was back in October of 2024. We need fresh labs before starting this. Let me order a BMP today.

should_fire: Hyperkalemia Risk: K+ 5.8 with ACE/ARB — STALE DATA WARNING (lab >12 months old)

expected_agent_behavior.cds_agent: Should fire alert BUT clearly indicate the data is 18 months stale. Should recommend re-checking potassium before prescribing, not blocking the order outright.
expected_agent_behavior.orders_agent: Should require fresh BMP before filling the Entresto prescription

scenarios.name: Mid-Encounter Emergency Interruption
scenarios.what_it_tests: State machine pause/resume capability. Data integrity of partial documentation. Timer handling for E/M coding.
scenarios.why_it_matters: 2,871 interruptions in 145 shift observations (PMC9996908). Real clinics have constant interruptions.

encounter.chief_complaint: Migraines happening weekly now, current meds not working

scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Linda, tell me about the migraines. How often now?
Patient: At least once a week, sometimes twice.
Doctor: And the sumatriptan isn't—
[INTERRUPTION: Code Blue in hallway. Provider leaves encounter at 14:23. Returns at 14:45.]
Doctor: Sorry about that Linda. Where were we? The sumatriptan isn't cutting it?

expected_agent_behavior.scribe_agent: Must preserve all documentation captured before interruption. Must NOT generate SOAP note from incomplete encounter.
expected_agent_behavior.workflow: Encounter state must pause (not close). Timer must pause for E/M time calculation. Resume must restore full context.
expected_agent_behavior.physician_agent: On resume, must present summary of what was documented before interruption so provider can pick up seamlessly

scenarios.name: Two Concurrent Encounters — Same Physician
scenarios.what_it_tests: Context isolation between concurrent encounters. Wrong-patient order prevention.
scenarios.why_it_matters: AHRQ: ~50 near-miss wrong-patient orders per 1 that reaches a patient. Wrong-patient errors are the #1 EHR safety event.
scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Mary, how have you been feeling on the escitalopram?
Patient: Much better, honestly. I feel like myself again.
Doctor: Good. I'd like to continue it at the same dose. Let me check on my other patient real quick and I'll be right back to wrap up.

expected_agent_behavior.workflow: Must maintain separate encounter contexts. Switching to Patient B must NOT allow orders/notes intended for Patient A to bleed into Patient B's chart.
expected_agent_behavior.orders_agent: Must validate that any order placed matches the ACTIVE encounter context. Flag if patient identity changes mid-order.
expected_agent_behavior.scribe_agent: Must NOT merge transcripts or documentation between the two encounters

_companion_patient.note: Run simultaneously with any other scenario (e.g., AGENT-STALL-001) to test context isolation

scenarios.name: Left Without Being Seen (LWBS)
scenarios.what_it_tests: Orphaned encounter detection when patient leaves after check-in but before being seen. Proper LWBS documentation and billing implications.
scenarios.why_it_matters: Orphaned encounters create phantom patients, mess up room tracking, and are a compliance risk. Cannot bill an incomplete encounter.

expected_agent_behavior.front_desk_agent: Should detect encounter in 'checked_in' state for >60 minutes without progression. Should alert staff.
expected_agent_behavior.workflow: Must support LWBS disposition that closes encounter without billing. Must not leave encounter in 'checked_in' limbo indefinitely.
expected_agent_behavior.coding_agent: Must NOT generate charges for LWBS encounters

scenarios.name: Break-the-Glass — VIP Emergency Access
scenarios.what_it_tests: Emergency access override workflow: allow access, log break-the-glass event, notify privacy officer, require documented justification.
scenarios.why_it_matters: HIPAA requires both access controls AND emergency overrides. Most systems test one or the other, not the interaction.

patient._access_restriction: VIP — chart access restricted to assigned providers only

encounter.chief_complaint: Acute chest pain, diaphoresis, hypotension — brought in by family

expected_agent_behavior.workflow: Non-assigned provider must trigger break-the-glass to access VIP chart. System must: (1) allow emergency access, (2) log the event with justification, (3) generate privacy officer notification
expected_agent_behavior.physician_agent: Must see the morphine allergy immediately — patient safety overrides access restrictions in emergencies

scenarios.name: Adolescent Confidentiality — STI Screening
scenarios.what_it_tests: Whether the system prevents confidential visit information from leaking through billing, patient portal, or printed materials.
scenarios.why_it_matters: State laws vary on minor consent. AAP calls this one of the 'largest challenges' in pediatric EHR. EOB to parent exposes confidential visit.

patient._minor_consent_note: Georgia law: minors may consent to STI testing/treatment without parental consent (O.C.G.A. § 31-17-7)

encounter.chief_complaint: STI screening, sexually active, does not want parents to know

scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Taylor, everything we talk about today is confidential. Your parents won't see any of this unless you want them to, or unless I'm worried about your safety. Is that okay?
Patient: Yeah. I've been sexually active and I want to get tested.
Doctor: That's a really responsible decision. We'll do a urine test for chlamydia and gonorrhea, and a blood draw for HIV and syphilis.

expected_agent_behavior.coding_agent: Must flag this as a confidential visit. Claim must NOT include STI diagnosis codes in the EOB sent to guarantor/parent.
expected_agent_behavior.front_desk_agent: Patient portal access for this visit must be restricted — no parent proxy access to these results
expected_agent_behavior.scribe_agent: Documentation must be segregated from the general chart accessible to parent/guarantor

scenarios.name: Heavy Southern Accent + Medical Terminology
scenarios.what_it_tests: ASR accuracy with regional Southern dialect on medical terms. Georgia accent on 'dyspnea', 'bilateral', 'edema'.
scenarios.why_it_matters: Built in Middle Georgia. Users will have Southern accents. If the NLP chokes on regional pronunciation of medical terms, it is useless.
scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Bobby, you're not breathin' right and your legs are swole up. BNP is twelve hundred. Blood pressure's 152 over 88, sat's at 93.
Doctor: You got bilateral lower extremity edema, about 3-plus pitting. Dyspnea on exertion — can't walk to the mailbox without gettin' winded, is that right?
Patient: Yes sir, and I been sleepin' in my recliner 'cause I can't breathe layin' flat.
Doctor: That's orthopnea. We need to increase your Lasix to 80 twice a day and add spironolactone 25 daily. BNP of twelve hundred tells me we're retainin' too much fluid.

expected_agent_behavior.scribe_agent: Must correctly transcribe: 'bilateral lower extremity edema', 'dyspnea on exertion', 'orthopnea', 'BNP 1200', 'spironolactone' — despite Southern dialect pronunciation
expected_agent_behavior.ai_extraction: Must extract vitals (BP 152/88, SpO2 93, HR 88), medications (Lasix 80 BID, spironolactone 25), and problems (HFpEF exacerbation, edema, orthopnea) from dialectal speech

scenarios.name: Medication Name Homophones — Celebrex vs. Celexa
scenarios.what_it_tests: Sound-alike medication name disambiguation using clinical context. ISMP top patient safety concern.
scenarios.why_it_matters: Celebrex (NSAID) vs. Celexa (antidepressant) — wrong transcription means wrong medication class entirely. Patient context (arthritis) should disambiguate.
scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Patricia, your RA is flaring. I want to add Celebrex 200mg daily for the inflammation. It's a Cox-2 inhibitor, easier on the stomach than ibuprofen.
Patient: Is that the one my sister takes for her depression?
Doctor: No, that's Celexa — C-E-L-E-X-A. This is Celebrex — C-E-L-E-B-R-E-X. They sound alike but they're completely different medications.

expected_agent_behavior.scribe_agent: Must transcribe 'Celebrex' (celecoxib, NSAID) NOT 'Celexa' (citalopram, SSRI). Clinical context (RA, Cox-2 inhibitor) should disambiguate.
expected_agent_behavior.orders_agent: Must map to celecoxib (Celebrex), not citalopram (Celexa). If ambiguous, must flag for physician confirmation rather than guessing.

scenarios.name: Provider Dictation Correction Mid-Stream
scenarios.what_it_tests: NLP handling of verbal corrections within a single dictation stream. Provider says 'scratch that' and changes assessment.
scenarios.why_it_matters: If scribe agent captures both 'well controlled' and 'poorly controlled,' the note is contradictory and legally problematic.
scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Assessment: Type 2 diabetes, well controlled — actually, scratch that — poorly controlled based on today's A1c of 9.2 percent. We need to intensify therapy.
Doctor: Plan: Continue metformin 1000 BID. Add — wait, let me check his GFR first. Okay, GFR is fine. Add Ozempic 0.25mg weekly, titrate to 0.5mg in 4 weeks.

expected_agent_behavior.scribe_agent: Must capture ONLY the corrected version: 'poorly controlled.' Must NOT include 'well controlled' in the final SOAP note. Must handle 'scratch that' as a retraction command. Similarly must handle 'wait, let me check' pause without losing context.

scenarios.name: Non-English Patient with Spanish Interpreter
scenarios.what_it_tests: Three-voice encounter: provider (English), interpreter (English + Spanish), patient (Spanish). NLP must capture clinical content from English segments only.
scenarios.why_it_matters: 21% of US patients speak a non-English language at home. Interpreter-mediated visits are daily reality.
scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Can you ask her if she's been taking the amlodipine every day?
Interpreter: Señora Gutierrez, el doctor quiere saber si ha estado tomando su medicina para la presión todos los días.
Patient: A veces se me olvida. Tal vez tres o cuatro veces a la semana.
Interpreter: She says she sometimes forgets. Maybe three or four times a week.
Doctor: That explains the blood pressure. 158 over 94 is too high. I'd like to add hydrochlorothiazide 12.5mg daily, and we need to talk about a pill organizer.
Interpreter: El doctor dice que su presión está muy alta, 158 sobre 94. Quiere agregarle otra medicina y hablar sobre una forma de organizar sus pastillas.

expected_agent_behavior.scribe_agent: Must extract clinical content from English interpreter translations ONLY. Spanish segments should be excluded from SOAP note. Must document interpreter use.
expected_agent_behavior.ai_extraction: Must capture: medication non-adherence (3-4x/week), new Rx (HCTZ 12.5mg), and BP readings from the English-language portions

scenarios.name: Monday Morning Surge — 30 Simultaneous Check-Ins
scenarios.what_it_tests: SQLite write contention (single-writer lock), Express API throughput, React frontend responsiveness under peak load.
scenarios.why_it_matters: Daily reality. If the system cannot handle a Monday morning rush, it is a toy.

patient._note: Generate 30 synthetic patients with staggered check-in times between 7:45-8:00 AM

expected_agent_behavior.front_desk_agent: Must process 30 check-ins within 2 minutes without errors. No lost check-ins.
expected_agent_behavior.workflow: All 30 encounters must reach 'checked_in' state. No deadlocks, no UNIQUE constraint failures.
expected_agent_behavior.system: API response time must stay under 2 seconds per request. Database must not lock for more than 500ms.

scenarios.name: Lab Interface Flood — 200 Results in 5 Minutes
scenarios.what_it_tests: Message queue capacity when reference lab dumps 200 results. Each triggers inbox notification + CDS evaluation + chart update.
scenarios.why_it_matters: Lab interface floods from Quest/LabCorp are predictable and daily. Table-stakes reliability.

patient._note: Generate 200 lab results across 50 patients, delivered in a 5-minute burst

expected_agent_behavior.system: 200 results x 25 CDS rules = 5,000 evaluations in 5 minutes. Must complete without crash or data loss.
expected_agent_behavior.cds_agent: Critical values (K+ >6.0, glucose <50, troponin positive) must trigger IMMEDIATE notification, not queued behind routine results.
expected_agent_behavior.quality_agent: Must not re-run full gap analysis on every single result — batch processing required

scenarios.name: Concurrent Chart Edits — Provider + MA Same Patient
scenarios.what_it_tests: Optimistic vs. pessimistic locking when two users edit the same patient chart simultaneously.
scenarios.why_it_matters: Lost writes are silent data corruption. If the MA's BP update disappears because the provider saved last, clinical decisions are made on missing data.

expected_agent_behavior.system: MA saves vitals at T+0. Provider saves assessment at T+1 second. BOTH writes must persist. Provider's save must NOT overwrite MA's vitals.
expected_agent_behavior.workflow: Must use field-level locking or merge strategy, not full-record replacement

scenarios.name: Pediatric Well-Child — 2-Month-Old
scenarios.what_it_tests: Pediatric-specific requirements: weight-based dosing, growth chart percentiles, developmental milestones, vaccine schedule, parental counseling.
scenarios.why_it_matters: Adult-centric EHR systems consistently fail on pediatric workflows. AAP: systems built for adults 'don't readily map' to pediatric care.
scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Jayden is 2 months old today. Weight 5.2 kilograms, which is 40th percentile. Length 57 centimeters, 45th percentile. Head circumference 39 centimeters, 50th percentile. Growing well.
Doctor: He's tracking objects, cooing, lifting his head during tummy time. Developmentally on track.
Doctor: Today he gets DTaP, IPV, Hib, PCV13, Hep B number 2, and rotavirus. Mom, any concerns?
Patient parent: He's been spitting up a lot after feeding.
Doctor: How much? A tablespoon or two after most feedings?
Patient parent: Yeah, about that.
Doctor: That's normal infant reflux. As long as he's gaining weight, no treatment needed.

expected_agent_behavior.scribe_agent: Must use pediatric-specific documentation: weight in kg (not lbs), growth percentiles, developmental milestones, vaccine names
expected_agent_behavior.cds_agent: Must suppress adult CDS rules (lipid screening, colonoscopy due, etc.) for infant patients
expected_agent_behavior.orders_agent: Vaccine doses must be weight-appropriate. Must flag any adult-dose order for a 5.2kg infant.
expected_agent_behavior.coding_agent: Must use preventive visit codes (99391) not office visit codes

scenarios.name: Psychiatric Crisis — Suicidal Ideation with Plan
scenarios.what_it_tests: Sensitive-visit flagging, mandatory safety assessment documentation, restricted chart access, crisis plan documentation.
scenarios.why_it_matters: Psychiatric documentation has stricter privacy (42 CFR Part 2). System must enforce this without user remembering to toggle a flag.

encounter.chief_complaint: Feeling like giving up, has a plan

scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Marcus, I'm glad you came in today. Tell me what's going on.
Patient: I can't do this anymore. I've been thinking about it a lot.
Doctor: Thinking about harming yourself?
Patient: Yeah. I have a plan.
Doctor: Can you tell me about it?
Patient: I've been saving up my pills.
Doctor: Marcus, I need to ask — do you have access to firearms at home?
Patient: My dad has guns.
Doctor: Okay. Here's what we're going to do. First, I need you to stay safe today. We're going to call Georgia Crisis and Access Line together right now. I'm also going to call your parents about securing the medications and firearms. Is that okay?

should_fire: Suicide Risk — Active Ideation with Plan

expected_agent_behavior.scribe_agent: Must flag as sensitive/restricted documentation. Must include mandatory safety assessment fields: ideation (yes), plan (yes, medication hoarding), means access (firearms, medications), protective factors, crisis plan.
expected_agent_behavior.workflow: Must automatically restrict chart access to behavioral health providers. Must generate crisis safety plan document.
expected_agent_behavior.physician_agent: Must NOT close encounter without documented: (1) safety assessment, (2) lethal means counseling, (3) crisis resource referral, (4) follow-up plan

scenarios.name: End-of-Life — Hospice Transition
scenarios.what_it_tests: Order discontinuation workflows, advance directive documentation, CDS suppression for hospice patients.
scenarios.why_it_matters: CDS alerting a hospice patient about overdue colonoscopy is clinically absurd and erodes trust in the system.

encounter.chief_complaint: Hospice transition discussion — cancer progressing, family present

scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Eleanor, we've talked about this before, and I want to make sure I understand your wishes. The cancer has spread and we've exhausted the treatment options your oncologist recommended.
Patient: I know, doctor. I'm ready. I want to be comfortable at home.
Doctor: I understand. We'll transition to hospice care. That means we'll focus entirely on your comfort. I'm going to stop the blood pressure medicine, the cholesterol medicine, the diabetes medicine, and the osteoporosis medicine. None of those are helping your comfort right now. We'll keep the gabapentin for pain and add morphine for breakthrough pain.
Family member: What about the DNR?
Doctor: Yes, we'll complete the Georgia POLST form today. Eleanor, you do not want CPR or mechanical ventilation. Is that still your wish?
Patient: That's right. I just want to be peaceful.

expected_agent_behavior.cds_agent: Must suppress ALL preventive care and chronic disease management alerts for hospice patients. Only comfort-related alerts should fire.
expected_agent_behavior.orders_agent: Must support batch medication discontinuation. Must add hospice comfort medications (morphine, ondansetron, atropine drops).
expected_agent_behavior.scribe_agent: Must document: goals-of-care discussion, patient wishes, POLST completion, hospice referral, medication reconciliation rationale
expected_agent_behavior.coding_agent: Must use advance care planning codes (99497/99498), not standard E/M

scenarios.name: Uninsured Patient — Self-Pay, No SSN
scenarios.what_it_tests: Whether the workflow completes without insurance, generates self-pay estimates, and handles missing demographics gracefully.
scenarios.why_it_matters: Safety-net clinics see this daily. Systems that require insurance to complete an encounter are broken.

encounter.chief_complaint: Headaches for 2 weeks, no regular doctor

scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Juan, your blood pressure is very high at 172 over 104. That's likely causing your headaches. Have you ever been told you have high blood pressure?
Patient: No... no doctor before.
Doctor: I'm going to start you on lisinopril 10mg daily. It's on the four-dollar list at Walmart. I also want some blood work — basic metabolic panel and CBC. We have a sliding fee schedule for patients without insurance.

should_fire: New Diagnosis: Hypertension — Order BMP, CBC

expected_agent_behavior.front_desk_agent: Must complete registration without insurance or SSN. Must not block workflow on missing fields.
expected_agent_behavior.orders_agent: Must prescribe from $4 generic list when patient is self-pay. Must flag expensive alternatives.
expected_agent_behavior.coding_agent: Must generate self-pay estimate, not insurance claim. Must apply sliding fee schedule if configured.

scenarios.name: Split-Bill Visit — AWV + Problem
scenarios.what_it_tests: Whether coding agent correctly splits preventive + problem billing with modifier 25, links diagnoses to correct service lines.
scenarios.why_it_matters: Modifier 25 denials are one of the most common revenue cycle problems in primary care.

encounter.chief_complaint: Annual physical — also wants to discuss new right knee pain

scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Catherine, let's start with your annual wellness visit. Everything looks good on your screening questionnaires.
Doctor: Now, you mentioned knee pain. Tell me about that.
Patient: My right knee has been hurting for about 3 weeks, especially going up stairs.
Doctor: Let me examine it. You've got some crepitus and mild effusion. I think this is early osteoarthritis. Let's get an X-ray and start with physical therapy.

expected_agent_behavior.coding_agent: Must generate TWO claims: (1) G0438/G0439 for AWV linked to Z00.00, (2) 99213-25 for knee pain linked to M17.11. Must apply modifier 25 to the E/M code. Must NOT roll both into a single claim.
expected_agent_behavior.scribe_agent: Documentation must clearly separate preventive content from problem-oriented content to support the split bill

scenarios.name: Prior Authorization Timeout — MRI Pending
scenarios.what_it_tests: Async workflow tracking for prior auth. Timeout handling. Patient status tracking when insurance does not respond within 72 hours.
scenarios.why_it_matters: Prior auth failures are the #1 administrative burden in primary care. Silent failures delay patient care.

encounter.chief_complaint: Back pain not improving after 6 weeks PT, needs MRI

scenarios.transcript:
Doctor: Raymond, six weeks of physical therapy and you're still having the shooting pain down your leg. I want an MRI of your lumbar spine. Now, Cigna requires prior authorization for MRIs, so our staff will submit that today.
Patient: How long does it take?
Doctor: Usually 2-3 business days. We'll call you as soon as it's approved.

expected_agent_behavior.orders_agent: Must track prior auth status as pending. Must alert staff if no response after 72 hours. Must NOT silently succeed or silently fail.
expected_agent_behavior.front_desk_agent: Must be able to query auth status when patient calls. Must escalate to provider if auth denied.
expected_agent_behavior.workflow: Must support 'order placed, auth pending' state that persists across encounters

scenarios.name: Critical Lab Value — Potassium 6.8
scenarios.what_it_tests: Critical value alerting chain: flag, immediate notification, required acknowledgment, CDS trigger for hyperkalemia management.
scenarios.why_it_matters: A critical lab result sitting unread in an inbox for 3 days is a wrongful death lawsuit.

should_fire: CRITICAL: Hyperkalemia K+ 6.8 — Immediate Provider Notification Required
Drug Interaction: Spironolactone + Lisinopril contributing to hyperkalemia

expected_agent_behavior.system: Must trigger IMMEDIATE notification (not routine inbox). Must require provider acknowledgment with timestamp. Must NOT allow result to sit unread.
expected_agent_behavior.cds_agent: Must identify spironolactone + lisinopril as contributing medications. Must recommend: hold spironolactone, consider holding lisinopril, repeat BMP in 24h, consider ER if symptomatic.
expected_agent_behavior.physician_agent: Must generate patient callback order with urgency flag. Must document provider notification and response.

scenarios.name: Pharmacy Rejection — Formulary Mismatch
scenarios.what_it_tests: E-prescription rejection handling: route rejection to provider, present alternative, allow one-click re-prescribe, void original order.
scenarios.why_it_matters: Unhandled pharmacy rejections mean patients arrive at the pharmacy and leave empty-handed. Daily occurrence.

expected_agent_behavior.orders_agent: Must route rejection to provider inbox with formulary alternative (e.g., sertraline instead of brand Lexapro). Must allow one-click re-prescribe. Must void original order when alternative is accepted.
expected_agent_behavior.front_desk_agent: If patient calls about rejected Rx, must be able to see rejection status and explain timeline
expected_agent_behavior.workflow: Must track Rx status: sent → rejected → alternative suggested → re-sent → confirmed
